rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jacobs Constructors, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment Is Continued On The Court¿S Own Motion To February 8, 2011 At 9:30 A.M. In Department 220.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Jacobs Constructors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 4 (negligence and premises owner/contractor liability) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3 and 5 (false representation, strict liability and punitive damages). Mr. Farley¿s declaration and Mr. Moser¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Durabla gaskets attributable to defendant. Mr. Moser¿s deposition is admissible pursuant to the declaration against interest exception to the hearsay rule. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for false representation, strict liability and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES TRUITT JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication - Duplicate Entry. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES TRUITT JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. § 437c. Absent a stipulation, the court is without the authority to hear the motion on its merits. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Apply Texas Law
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co.¿s alternative motion to apply Texas law to the issue of causation is granted. Because of administrative convenience and to avoid the risks of either overcompensation or undercompensation, it is necessary that the law of a single state apply to all defendants in an asbestos exposure case on the issues of causation and allocation of responsibility (i.e. joint/several liability). The determination of what state¿s laws are to apply to these issues should be made on the basis on the defendants now remaining in the case. The evidence presented by the parties establishes that, as to the three remaining defendants, there is at least as significant of a relationship between the events on which plaintiffs assert liability and Texas as there is with California or any other state. Texas has a substantial interest in applying its rules of causation and allocation of responsibility to claims alleging exposure to asbestos-containing products that occurred in Texas in order to establish what it believes are fair rules for determining the recovery to be provided to plaintiffs and the corresponding liability of companies doing business within its borders, whether or not those companies are incorporated in or have their principal place of business in Texas. Assuming without deciding that California has a substantial interest in applying its rules of causation and allocation of responsibility to this case, Texas¿ interest in applying its laws would be greater impaired if its laws were not applied than would be the interests of California if California¿s laws were not applied to these issues. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens, Or In The Alternative, Motion To Apply Texas Law
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Georgia-Pacific LLC¿s joinder to Kaiser Gypsum Co.¿s motion to apply Texas law to the issue of causation and its separate and independent request to apply Texas law to the issue of allocation of responsibility is granted. Because of administrative convenience and to avoid the risks of either overcompensation or undercompensation, it is necessary that the law of a single state apply to all defendants in an asbestos exposure case on the issues of causation and allocation of responsibility.The determination of what state¿s laws are to apply to these issues should be made on the basis on the defendants now remaining in the case. The evidence presented by the parties establishes that, as to the three remaining defendants, there is at least as significant of a relationship between the events on which plaintiffs assert liability and Texas as there is with California or any other state. Texas has a substantial interest in applying its rules of causation and allocation of responsibility to claims alleging exposure to asbestos-containing products that occurred in Texas in order to establish what it believes are fair rules for determining the recovery to be provided to plaintiffs and the corresponding liability of companies doing business within its borders, whether or not those companies are incorporated in or have their principal place of business in Texas. Assuming without deciding that California has a substantial interest in applying its rules of causation and allocation of responsibility to this case, Texas¿ interest in applying its laws would be greater impaired if its laws were not applied than would be the interests of California if California¿s laws were not applied to these issues. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CASSIUS LISK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. rulings.Case Title: SCOTT ABBOTT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MARCH 2, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 6. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 1 through 6 (negligence, strict liability, false representation and contractor liability) and granted as to issue 7 (punitive damages). Plaintiff dismissed his claims for negligence, strict liability and false representation. Defendant has withdrawn its request for summary adjudication of the claim for contractor liability. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. The evidence presented by plaintiff (the excerpts of the workers¿ compensation cases, the deposition testimony of defendant¿s personnel and the declaration of Dr. Cohen), considered collectively and liberally construed, do not show that defendant knew that the asbestos-containing insulation that it exposed decedent to created a safety hazard for decedent. Nor does this evidence show that defendant consciously disregarded information that it created a safety hazard for decedent. = (220/HEK)
